---
title: "Daily Models"
output: html_document
---

# setup
```{r}
library(here)
library(tseries)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(cowplot)
library(forecast)
library(gt)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

# Load in the data
```{r}
#load in data
#ignore my wd. chunk was overriding what I set in the console, # out if not Sadie
setwd("/Users/sadiekasten/Desktop/Time Series/R files/final proj")
raw_Std <- read.csv(file="data/processed/Std_household_load.csv",
                            header=TRUE) %>%
  mutate(datetime = ymd_hms(datetime_utc, tz = "UTC")) %>% 
  mutate(cluster = as.character(cluster)) %>% 
  select("datetime","cluster","load_kwh") %>% 
  mutate(cluster = paste("Std",cluster))


raw_ToU <- read.csv(file="data/processed/ToU_household_load.csv",
                            header=TRUE) %>%
  mutate(datetime = ymd_hms(datetime_utc, tz = "UTC")) %>% 
  mutate(cluster = as.character(cluster)) %>% 
  select("datetime","cluster","load_kwh") %>% 
  mutate(cluster = paste("ToU",cluster))
```


#Process data into hourly dataframes

```{r}
processed_Std_2012 <- raw_Std %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cluster,
              values_from = load_kwh) %>% 
  select("datetime","Std 1","Std 2") %>% 
  filter(year(datetime) == 2012)

daily_Std_2012 <- processed_Std_2012 %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(datetime)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    `Std 1_avg` = mean(`Std 1`, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Std 2_avg` = mean(`Std 2`, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

processed_ToU_2012 <- raw_ToU %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cluster,
              values_from = load_kwh) %>% 
  select("datetime","ToU 1","ToU 2") %>% 
  filter(year(datetime) == 2012)

daily_ToU_2012 <- processed_ToU_2012 %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(datetime)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    `ToU 1_avg` = mean(`ToU 1`, na.rm = TRUE),
    `ToU 2_avg` = mean(`ToU 2`, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

processed_Std_2013 <- raw_Std %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cluster,
              values_from = load_kwh) %>% 
  select("datetime","Std 1","Std 2") %>% 
  filter(year(datetime) == 2013)

daily_Std_2013 <- processed_Std_2013 %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(datetime)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    `Std 1_avg` = mean(`Std 1`, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Std 2_avg` = mean(`Std 2`, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

processed_ToU_2013 <- raw_ToU %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cluster,
              values_from = load_kwh) %>% 
  select("datetime","ToU 1","ToU 2") %>% 
  filter(year(datetime) == 2013)

daily_ToU_2013 <- processed_ToU_2013 %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(datetime)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    `ToU 1_avg` = mean(`ToU 1`, na.rm = TRUE),
    `ToU 2_avg` = mean(`ToU 2`, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")
```


#Convert to daily ts
```{r}

#2012
ts_Std1_2012_d <- msts(daily_Std_2012$`Std 1_avg`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(7, 365.25),
                            start=c(2012,1,0)) 

ts_Std2_2012_d <- msts(daily_Std_2012$`Std 2_avg`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(7, 365.25),
                            start=c(2012,1,0)) 

ts_ToU1_2012_d <- msts(daily_ToU_2012$`ToU 1_avg`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(7, 365.25),
                            start=c(2012,1,0)) 

ts_ToU2_2012_d <- msts(daily_ToU_2012$`ToU 2_avg`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(7, 365.25),
                            start=c(2012,1,0)) 

#2013

ts_Std1_2013_d <- msts(daily_Std_2013$`Std 1_avg`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(7, 365.25),
                            start=c(2013,1,0)) 

ts_Std2_2013_d <- msts(daily_Std_2013$`Std 2_avg`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(7, 365.25),
                            start=c(2013,1,0)) 

ts_ToU1_2013_d <- msts(daily_ToU_2013$`ToU 1_avg`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(7, 365.25),
                            start=c(2013,1,0)) 

ts_ToU2_2013_d <- msts(daily_ToU_2013$`ToU 2_avg`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(7, 365.25),
                            start=c(2013,1,0))

```


#ARIMA Model
```{r}
#ARIMA + Fourier Terms for Std1 2012 training time series
#Annual, weekly, and daily seasonality likely for electricity load data.
#hourly load forecasting
#using less than a year of data to test, not going to include yearly K value >1
#training subset, using December of 2012, hourly (744)
n_dec = 31
ts_Std1_train <- subset(ts_Std1_2012_d, end = length(ts_Std1_2012_d)-n_dec)

ARIMA_Std1_train <- auto.arima(ts_Std1_train, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_Std1_train,  K=c(2,12)))
AIC(ARIMA_Std1_train)

#Forecast the ARIMA model
ARIMAF_Std1_train_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_Std1_train,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_Std1_train, K=c(2,12),h=31), h=31) 

#Plot forecast
Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_daily <- autoplot(ARIMAF_Std1_train_forecast) + ylab("Load (kWh)")
print(Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_daily)

#Plot forecast on top of actual
Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_actual_daily <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2012_d) +
  autolayer(ARIMAF_Std1_train_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)")
print(Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_actual_daily)
```


```{r}
#ARIMA + Fourier Terms for all Std1 for 2013

ARIMA_Std1 <- auto.arima(ts_Std1_2012_d, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_Std1_2012_d,  K=c(2,12)))

ARIMA_Std1_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_Std1,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_Std1_2012_d, K=c(2,12),h=365), h=365) 

ARIMA_Std1_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2013_d) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_Std1_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="Forecast vs Actual Std1")
print(ARIMA_Std1_plot)
```

I mean it could be worse. This looks a lot better than the hourly data anyway.
Might be better if temp is added as an exogenous variable.

```{r}
#The other 3 groups + visualizations
#Std2
ARIMA_Std2 <- auto.arima(ts_Std2_2012_d, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_Std2_2012_d,  K=c(2,12)))

ARIMA_Std2_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_Std2,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_Std2_2012_d, K=c(2,12),h=365), h=365) 

ARIMA_Std2_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std2_2013_d) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_Std2_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="Forecast vs Actual Std2")

#ToU1
ARIMA_ToU1 <- auto.arima(ts_ToU1_2012_d, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_ToU1_2012_d,  K=c(2,12)))

ARIMA_ToU1_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_ToU1,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_ToU1_2012_d, K=c(2,12),h=365), h=365) 

ARIMA_ToU1_plot <- autoplot(ts_ToU1_2013_d) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_ToU1_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="Forecast vs Actual ToU1")

#ToU2
ARIMA_ToU2 <- auto.arima(ts_ToU2_2012_d, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_ToU2_2012_d,  K=c(2,12)))

ARIMA_ToU2_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_ToU2,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_ToU2_2012_d, K=c(2,12),h=365), h=365) 

ARIMA_ToU2_plot <- autoplot(ts_ToU2_2013_d) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_ToU2_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="Forecast vs Actual ToU2")

#ARIMA Plot visualization
ARIMA_F_2013_plots_daily <- plot_grid(ARIMA_Std1_plot,
          ARIMA_Std2_plot,
          ARIMA_ToU1_plot,
          ARIMA_ToU2_plot,
          nrow =2, ncol = 2)
print(ARIMA_F_2013_plots_daily)
```


```{r}
#accuracy scores for ARIMA-Fourier models
ARIMAF_Std1_accuracy <- accuracy(ARIMA_Std1_forecast$mean,ts_Std1_2013_d)

ARIMAF_Std2_accuracy <- accuracy(ARIMA_Std2_forecast$mean,ts_Std2_2013_d)

ARIMAF_ToU1_accuracy <- accuracy(ARIMA_ToU1_forecast$mean,ts_ToU1_2013_d)

ARIMAF_ToU2_accuracy <- accuracy(ARIMA_ToU2_forecast$mean,ts_ToU2_2013_d)

#scores dataframe
ARIMAF_Scores <- as.data.frame(rbind(ARIMAF_Std1_accuracy, ARIMAF_Std2_accuracy, ARIMAF_ToU1_accuracy, ARIMAF_ToU2_accuracy))
row.names(ARIMAF_Scores) <- c("Std1", "Std2","ToU1", "ToU2")

print(ARIMAF_Scores)
```

```{r}
#TBATS for Std1
TBATS_Std1_daily <- tbats(ts_Std1_2012_d)

TBATS_Std1_forecast <- forecast(TBATS_Std1_daily, h=365)

print(Std1_TBATS_2013_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2013_d) +
  autolayer(TBATS_Std1_forecast, series="TBATS",PI=FALSE)+
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="TBATS Std1"))
```
